picks.title: Homelanding
picks.blurb: A free summer festival on the waterfront Marineterrein — food, music, a swim spot and design across the old naval yard.

picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: POISED w/ Force Reaction, Oxygeno, OZUM, Joey Marcel, Raeon
picks.blurb: Oxygeno, Force Reaction, Kaiser (K S R), Kwartz and more at TILLATEC.

picks.title: Madam by Night invites: VELVET
picks.blurb: HOTBOX COLLECTIVE, Lidia Lo, Sanne Dammers at Madam.

picks.title: Carel Visser in de Rijksmuseumtuinen
picks.blurb: Outdoor sculpture exhibition in museum gardens featuring Dutch modernist artist Carel Visser's abstract works.

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Major photography exhibition showcasing award-winning journalism and documentary work from around the world.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Monne
picks.blurb: Mediterranean restaurant with waterfront terrace, boat dock and seasonal cuisine in peaceful riverside setting.
picks.why: New Mediterranean spot with water views

picks.title: Nara Nara
picks.blurb: New Egyptian restaurant with shareable dishes celebrating Cairo flavors and Mediterranean influences.

picks.title: Garlic Festival
picks.blurb: Garlic-themed market with live music and food stalls, followed by three-course garlic dinner.

picks.title: Amsterdam Yard Sale Braderie
picks.blurb: Neighborhood flea market: 100 stalls of locals selling vintage items, clothes, curiosities.

picks.title: Hortus Zomeravonden
picks.blurb: Extended summer evenings in botanical gardens with guided tours highlighting rare plants, historic greenhouses, and hidden corners.
picks.why: Evening garden tours with live music

picks.title: The Sheep Detectives
picks.venue: Filmhallen, Pathé Bioscopen, Ketelhuis, Vué, Studio/K, The M
picks.blurb: Family comedy where clever sheep secretly solve a mystery on the farm after overhearing detective stories from their herder.
picks.why: Witty animated mystery for all ages

picks.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto
picks.blurb: Twenty years later, Miranda, Andy and colleagues navigate a transformed digital fashion world faster and harsher than ever.
picks.why: Iconic fashion drama returns after two decades

picks.title: Kay Slice QUANZA at Openluchttheater Vondelpark
picks.blurb: Free outdoor music performance in Vondelpark. Bring a picnic blanket and enjoy the summer evening entertainment.

picks.title: Rembrandt het leven
picks.blurb: Exhibition exploring Rembrandt's life and times in his former Amsterdam home studio.
picks.why: Master artist biography in historic home

picks.title: RadioRadio Oatly Oatly Pop-Up
picks.blurb: New summer hotspot with DJs from Amsterdam music scene, signature Oatly drinks, festival atmosphere, live radio broadcasts.

picks.title: Festival TREK
picks.blurb: Netherlands' largest traveling food festival transforms park into open-air restaurant with street food, theatre, live music, dance floor.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Your Little Black Book / I amsterdam

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Bostheater
picks.blurb: Open-air theater in Amsterdam's forest with diverse summer programming including theater, live music, and family shows.
picks.why: Unique outdoor theater in the forest

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Stoornis of my life
picks.blurb: Musical with Alex Klaasen about life, autism, and family. Hilarious, moving, and deeply relatable with live music and heart.
picks.why: Touching musical comedy with live music

picks.title: Julidans – International Contemporary Dance Festival
picks.blurb: International contemporary dance festival: innovative choreography, experimental avant-garde. Future of movement & expression.

picks.title: Pure Markt
picks.blurb:
Outing for the whole family
 Get your groceries for home, take a seat on one of the terraces to taste the offer in one of the most beautiful parks in Amsterdam,

picks.title: American Identities
picks.blurb:
David Levinthal 
 David Levinthal (born 1949) explores the role of myths in American visual culture. Using carefully constructed miniature scenes, the artist re

picks.title: BRET Club Night: Ciel, Jesse Maas, Julian Anthony, Caro
picks.blurb: House and techno in shipping-container venue. Industrial, red-walled club on Amsterdam's nightlife edge.

picks.title: Guilty Pleasure Festival
picks.blurb: Extravagant pop/dance festival with 90s/00s hits, sing-alongs. Final edition with Busta Rhymes lineup.

picks.title: Vunzige Deuntjes Festival
picks.blurb: Urban culture festival with 6 stages: hip hop, house, dancehall, disco. 20,000 visitors, live acts and DJs.
picks.why: Major day festival with diverse lineup

picks.title: Vondelpark Open Air Theatre
picks.blurb: Free open-air performances: music, dance, theatre, comedy. 50+ years of summer programming.

picks.title: Open Podium Noord
picks.blurb:
Accessible stage
 Open Podium Noord is free to attend. This makes the event accessible to everyone. The performances vary each week, depending on the artists wh

picks.title: De Roode Remise Singing Session
picks.blurb:
De Roode Remise Singing Session
 The core singers know each other from the famous Backroom Sessions at Mulligan's Irish Bar, but everyone, experienced or inexpe

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb:
Art, craft and design
 Museum Market takes place every month, every 3rd Sunday of the month (in October the 4th Sunday of the month). Between the trees near the

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Lindengrachtmarkt
picks.blurb: The Jordaan’s beloved Saturday market — farm cheese, flowers and Amsterdam’s best market-stall fish, all down a leafy street.

picks.title: Kinderdijk Windmills
picks.blurb: Nineteen 18th-century windmills lined along the polders — the postcard Dutch landscape, a UNESCO site you can walk or cycle.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch food films while eating exactly what appears on screen—brunch or seven-course dinner synchronized to scene transitions.

picks.title: The Jollof Club
picks.blurb: West African restaurant with modern cuisine inspired by Ghana, Nigeria and Senegal plus wine pairings.

picks.title: Throwback AMSTERDAM - Back to 80s, 90s & 00s
picks.blurb: Club night at Het Sieraad.

picks.title: Nicolas van Poucke - Pianorecital
picks.blurb:
Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke
 Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke returns to the Kleine Zaal, where he continues his remarkable recital tradition. Praised by

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End musical based on true story of Henry Fraser. Inspirational performance about life-changing moments and resilience.
